highlights.text: This got us long and I wrote at 4:20PM Monday “I have added long exposure a little after the close ~6396 as we put in a nice Failed Breakdown of the 6396 low set at 2:40PM.” I also wrote: “My general lean though is always to defer to the trend6398-6402 starts higher with 6418-20, then 6426 targets.
My lean was they could and I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “We’ve been paid massively and now there is litle to do but sit on runners and wait for price discovery(sideways basing or sharp sell).
My general lean is to defer to the trend.
As I frequently say, one of these building blocks is my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Failed Breakdowns don’t occur in a vacuum though.
I call this one the “elevator down” sell.
These sells stop when a Failed Breakdown triggers, and price squeezes.
Elevator down into Failed Breakdown into short squeeze.
ES sold off from 6430 then in the final minutes of trading Monday went “elevator down” to 6388.
We saw the cycle here: Elevator down, then violent squeeze.
This Failed Breakdown broke us out from a shelf of highs at 6426 we built from Thursday-Monday, and we were off.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the variety of Failed Breakdowns that built the scaffolding for this leg up (remember Failed Breakdowns = the footprint of institutional accumulation) and discuss how I traded them.

post_title: New All Time Highs For SPX. The Question Now; Can They Hold? August 14 Plan
